title: Bhindi Masala with Roti and Kachumber
description: Dry-ish okra masala with soft rotis and a fresh onion-tomato-cucumber salad—a complete everyday Indian plate.
explanation: Bhindi is often cooked as a side; this recipe treats it as the center of a full meal by pairing a generous sabzi portion with roti and kachumber.
Cooking bhindi uncovered and without excess water helps keep it less slimy. Onion and tomato build the base; salad adds crunch and volume.
ingredients: 300 g Bhindi (okra), washed, dried, and sliced
1/2 tsp Amchur or juice of 1/2 Lemon
For kachumber: 1 Cucumber, 1 Tomato, 1/2 Onion, lemon, salt, coriander
instructions: Pat bhindi completely dry after washing. Slice into rounds. Dice kachumber vegetables and toss with lemon, salt, and coriander; set aside.
Heat oil in a wide pan. Add cumin, then onion; saute until soft and lightly golden. Add ginger-garlic and cook until raw smell fades.
Add tomato, turmeric, coriander, chilli powder, and salt. Cook until tomato softens.
Add bhindi. Stir on medium heat 2–3 minutes, then cook uncovered, stirring often, until tender and the sticky strands reduce, 12–15 minutes.
Finish with amchur or lemon. Serve hot with rotis and a large scoop of kachumber.
stepNotes: Dry bhindi thoroughly—moisture is the main reason it turns slimy.
A wide pan is better than a deep pot so steam can escape.
Do not cover the pan for long once bhindi is in.
Amchur brightens the dish without making it watery.
Make rotis while bhindi cooks so the full plate lands together.

variationSections.title: Bhindi style swaps
items: Kurkuri-style: dust bhindi with a spoon of besan and roast drier.
Add capsicum strips in the last 5 minutes for more vegetables.
Use mustard oil for a stronger North Indian edge if you like it.

variationSections.title: Diet adjustments
items: Millet or jowar roti instead of wheat for a gluten-free plate (variation only).
Skip onion in kachumber and use extra cucumber and tomato if needed.
Add a small bowl of dal on the side when you want more protein.

nutrition.servingSize: 1 plate with 2 rotis and kachumber (half recipe)
